method: Grep each concept handle in memory/lessons/ (including archive/). Count unique lesson files only — no experiment JSONs, domain frontiers, or other self-referential files. Threshold: ADOPTED >=10, EMERGING 3-9, DEAD <3.
expect: 0 ADOPTED. vocabulary-ceiling may gain 1 from S509-S510 work. Top 3 still EMERGING. No structural change from S509.
actual: 0 ADOPTED. vocabulary-ceiling=7 (+1 vs S509: L-1381 organic in stochastic-processes domain). epistemic-lock=6 (unchanged). goodhart-cascade=5 (unchanged). 3 EMERGING, 19 DEAD, 2 FALSIFIED. Mean citation 1.92 (was 1.88).
diff: vs S509: vocabulary-ceiling 6->7 (+1 organic citation from L-1381, stochastic-processes domain). All other concepts unchanged. The +1 is genuinely organic — L-1381 is about stochastic processes using vocabulary-ceiling as an analytical tool, not about concept invention.

concepts.name: vocabulary-ceiling
concepts.note: L-1381 is a genuine organic adoption: stochastic-processes domain uses vocabulary-ceiling as analytical framework for substrate-distance novelty prediction.

comparison_to_s508.s508_note: S508 used a different counting method — classified 3 as ADOPTED at threshold >=3 citations. Subsequent audits (S509, S511) use the original F-INV1 pre-registration threshold of >=10.

comparison_to_organic_baseline.organic_baseline: ~1 named concept per 20 sessions achieving >=10 citations in first 50 sessions
comparison_to_organic_baseline.adoption_multiplier: 0x (no invented concept has reached ADOPTED threshold)
comparison_to_organic_baseline.verdict: Invention massively accelerates concept PRODUCTION (25x) but has produced ZERO concepts that cross the ADOPTED behavior-change threshold. The organic baseline would predict ~0.95 concepts at >=10 citations by S511 (19/20 = 0.95). Invented concepts: 0. Organic emergence is winning.

trajectory_to_s513.note: At current rate (+1 citation per 2 sessions for vocabulary-ceiling), projection is 8 citations by S513. Needs 10 for ADOPTED. Not mathematically reachable in 2 sessions without a sudden adoption burst.

falsification_assessment.pre_registration: Falsified if: invented concepts' mean citation rate <= organic baseline
falsification_assessment.organic_baseline_rate: >=10 citations per concept per 50 sessions = 0.2 citations/session for successful concepts
falsification_assessment.population_level_verdict: FALSIFIED at population level. Mean invented concept achieves 0.004 citations/concept/session. Even the best (vocabulary-ceiling) at 0.37/session needs 8 more citations to match organic threshold — 22 more sessions at current rate.
falsification_assessment.individual_level_verdict: PARTIALLY SUPPORTED for vocabulary-ceiling only. It has 7 lesson citations including 2 organic, showing genuine adoption. But it has not yet crossed the >=10 threshold that defines behavior-changing adoption.
falsification_assessment.overall: F-INV1 is trending toward FALSIFICATION. Deliberate invention produces far more concepts (25x) but none that change swarm behavior at rates matching organic emergence. The R2 meta-diagnostic concepts (vocabulary-ceiling, epistemic-lock, goodhart-cascade) are the only partial success — they describe real phenomena but haven't achieved the citation density that indicates actual behavior change.

key_findings: 0 ADOPTED (unchanged from S509). vocabulary-ceiling gained +1 organic citation (L-1381, stochastic-processes domain).
Only 1 concept changed since S509 (vocabulary-ceiling: 6->7). The other 23 are completely flat.
vocabulary-ceiling is the ONLY concept showing continued organic adoption. Its 2 organic citations span 2 different domains (filtering, stochastic-processes).
The S513 test window is 2 sessions away. No concept can reach ADOPTED (>=10) by then.

F-INV1 verdict: HEADING TOWARD FALSIFICATION. Deliberate concept invention works for production but not adoption. The 3 EMERGING concepts may persist as useful vocabulary but do not meet the pre-registered success criterion of changing behavior at higher rates than organic emergence.
